calculation_notes: The original queued uploads eventually completed and attempt 2 resumed from the completed expanded family VCF dataset.
Galaxy Filter1 selected the single recessive high-impact CFTR stop-gained family variant.
Galaxy Grep1 selected the matching ClinVar clinical record from the decompressed ClinVar reference.
The prompt-shaped CSV is derived only from those preserved Galaxy outputs.

direct_ground_truth_result_evaluation.basis: Direct comparison is not meaningful because the original Galaxy outputs are VCF/text evidence files rather than the prompt-shaped CSV ground-truth format.

checks.basis: Galaxy produced and preserved the family-filter candidate output and the ClinVar candidate output.
checks.name: galaxy_result_files_exist

checks.basis: The original Galaxy outputs are VCF/text evidence files, not the final requested CSV deliverable.
checks.name: single_prompt_csv

checks.basis: The original Galaxy outputs do not directly present the exact requested 16-column CSV header.
checks.name: required_prompt_columns

checks.basis: The preserved Galaxy outputs contain the selected CFTR G>T family variant and matching ClinVar record.
checks.name: causal_variant_present

transformed_ground_truth_result_evaluati.comparison_rule: Exact field comparison, except diseases are compared as semicolon/pipe-delimited sets because ClinVar and reference order disease labels differently.

checks.basis: The transformed helper is a single CSV.
checks.name: single_csv_deliverable

checks.basis: The transformed CSV contains exactly the requested 16 columns.
checks.name: required_columns

checks.basis: The transformed CSV contains exactly one causal candidate row.
checks.name: single_variant_row

checks.basis: The transformed CSV reports the selected CFTR causal variant.
checks.name: causal_variant_fields_present

transformed_prompt_result_evaluation.notes: The CSV parses and reorders fields from the Galaxy-filtered family VCF row and Galaxy-selected ClinVar row. Disease labels are carried from ClinVar order.
